ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS MAY PLEAD ALLEGATIONS OF PRODUCT LIABILITY AGAINST SEVEN RECENTLY IDENTIFIED DEFENDANTS BOSS MANUFACTURING COMPANY, GENUINE PARTS COMPANY, GEORGIA-PACIFIC LLC, HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL INC., ITT LLC, KELLY-MOORE PAINT COMPANY, INC. AND THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY. THEY MAY INCLUDE TWO NEW CAUSES OF ACTION FOR RESPIRATORY SAFETY DEVICE AND MAY NAME 3M COMPANY THEREUNDER. THEY MAY INCLUDE TWO NEW CAUSES OF ACTION FOR ASBESTOS BRAKE SHOE GRINDING MACHINE LIABILITY AND MAY NAME ACTUANT CORPORATION AND HENNESSY INDUSTRIES, LLC THEREUNDER. THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DEEMED SERVED AS OF THE DATE OF GRANTING THIS MOTION ON ALL DEFENDANTS WHO HAVE PREVIOUSLY APPEARED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CYNTHIA MING-MEI LEE, MATTER NOT REPORTED. (503)

ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON DEC-07-2017 IN 503, PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS AMENDED: GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 36(A) AND (D). TRIAL DATE IS MARCH 12, 2018 AT 11:15AM IN DEPARTMENT 503. THE LAST DAY PURSUANT TO CCP 36(F) IS APRIL 6, 2018. LOCAL RULE 20 SHALL BE FOLLOWED BY THE PARTIES AS WELL AS ALL SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULES AND CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT. THE LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTIONS IS MARCH 8, 2018. THE COURT WILL ALLOW A MAXIMUM OF FOUR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTIONS PER DAY TO BE CALENDARED IN THIS ACTION. SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IN ROOM 603 AT 9:30A.M. ON MARCH 8, 2018. FURTHER CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE ON JANUARY 4, 2018 AT 1:30P.M. NO NEED FOR ANY PARTY TO FILE ANY CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE STATEMENT. PARTIES MAY APPEAR AT THE CONFERENCE BY TELEPHONE. IF THE PARTIES AGREE THAT NO CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS NECESSARY, THEY SHALL NOTIFY THE COURT AT LEAST TWO DAY PRIOR TO TAKE THE CMC OFF CALENDAR. THERE WILL BE A MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE WITH THE COURT PURSUANT TO CRC, RULE 3.1380 AND SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULES OF COURT, RULES 5.0 AND 20.2 ON MARCH 12, 2018 AT 1:30P.M. IN DEPARTMENT 503. THE PARTIES SHALL COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULES 5.0 AND 20.2 AND PROVIDE A CONFIDENTIAL SETTLEMENT STATEMENT BY MARCH 1, 2018 AT 2:00P.M. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CYNTHIA MING-MEI LEE, REPORTER: DAVID A. DISBROW, CSR #7768 (TEL: 415-298-1538)

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as amended: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a) and (d). Trial date is March 12, 2018 at 11:15am in Department 503. This trial date is subject to the parties agreeing to the below conditions. If the parties do not agree, the Court will assign a trial date close to the 120-day mark. If the parties subsequently fail to comply with this order, the Court will entertain a motion to continue the trial date to a date close to the 120-day mark. The last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is April 6, 2018. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is March 8, 2018. The parties agree that summary judgment/adjudication motions may be brought on shortened notice. The notice period for such motions is shortened to 35 days. Opposition due 10 days before the hearing. Reply due 3 days before the hearing. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Plaintiffs must produce Mr. Mack for deposition so that his deposition concludes no later than January 12, 2018. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. (Mini Minutes Continued, see next entry)

Plaintiffs' Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff (Added To Calendar For Issuance Of Tentative Ruling Only.)
Off Calendar Dec-11-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
(PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini Minutes, continued from previous entry)

Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than January 26, 2018. Responses to the demand are due no later than February 9, 2018. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by March 5, 2018 at 2:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:30a.m. on March 8, 2018. Fact discovery cut-off date is February 23, 2018. Expert discovery cut-off date is March 9, 2018. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than December 29, 2017. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. No later than December 15, 2017 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than December 15, 2017 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on January 4, 2018 at 1:30p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to CRC, Rule 3.1380 and San Francisco Local Rules of Court, Rules 5.0 and 20.2 on March 12, 2018 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rules 5.0 and 20.2 and provide a confidential settlement statement by March 1, 2018 at 2:00p.m. The Court did not consider the untimely declaration of Elisabeth Mack. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: David A. Disbrow, CSR #7768 (Tel: 415-298-1538) (503/CML)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-06-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties M L Mack and Elizabeth Mack

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for order granting leave to file first amended complaint is granted. Plaintiffs may plead allegations of product liability against seven recently identified defendants Boss Manufacturing Company, Genuine Parts Company, Georgia-Pacific LLC, Honeywell International Inc., ITT LLC, Kelly-Moore Paint Company, Inc. and Thomas Dee Engineering Company. They may include two new causes of action for Respiratory Safety Device and may name 3M Company thereunder. They may include two new causes of action for Asbestos Brake Shoe Grinding Machine liability and may name Actuant Corporation and Hennessy Industries, LLC thereunder. The First Amended Complaint is deemed served as of the date of granting this motion on all defendants who have previously appeared. No opposition filed. Prevailing party to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, matter not reported (503/CML)
